cases.title: I’m not even shy to talk to the camera. I have a monetized YouTube channel. I post talking head videos on TikTok, so it’s not even an issue for me. I can literally pick up my phone and shoot. But the
cases.content:
I’m not even shy to talk to the camera. I have a monetized YouTube channel. I post talking head videos on TikTok, so it’s not even an issue for me. I can literally pick up my phone and shoot. But the issue is that, all platforms have different things and ways  you connect with your audience. The content I post here is specifically for this place, and that’s why I write here. I don’t think I can get my content from other platforms and post here. I don’t have same audience across all platforms. And even if I am forced to do that, is the payment method transparent enough? 

On my YouTube, I see my earnings per video and each time someone watches my content. I haven’t posted there for some months because of some reasons, which I will resume next month because my people already misses me, but each day I check my dashboard I see my money drop because people are still watching my videos daily. But this is not the same for x. The only thing I do on YouTube because I’m not fit to create currently is just to post few shorts, so my channel won’t be demonetized for shortage of watch hour. 
 
There’s no sustainability here, they can demonetize you at any point. I am currently paused, without no reason even after a long period of “below minimum earning”. So tell me why I should create talking head videos for this platform? No niche transparency. You don’t even know how much you earn per 1k views and how it’s been calculated. If una don tire just crash this app.

cases.title:
8/ The Monetization Accelerator

You are a YouTube monetization consultant who helps creators earn from their channels before and after hitting YPP requirements. I need every available revenue stream
cases.content:
8/ The Monetization Accelerator

You are a YouTube monetization consultant who helps creators earn from their channels before and after hitting YPP requirements. I need every available revenue stream activated in the right order.

Please provide:

-  Pre-monetization income: 5 ways to earn from YouTube before hitting 1,000 subscribers and 4,000 watch hours (affiliate links, digital products, consulting, Patreon, brand deals at small scale)
-  YPP fast-track: Specific content strategies that accumulate watch hours fastest — video length, topic type, and upload pattern that the algorithm rewards
-  AdSense optimization: How video length, topic, and audience demographics affect CPM and RPM, and how to make content choices that attract higher-paying ads
-  Sponsorship readiness: At what subscriber count and engagement rate to start pitching brands, what a media kit needs, and how to price sponsorships based on CPM
-  Affiliate strategy: How to identify the highest-commission affiliates in my niche, integrate them naturally into content, and disclose them compliantly
-  Digital product pipeline: How to use YouTube to validate and sell a course, ebook, template, or membership product to an existing audience
-  Channel membership design: What tiers to create, what perks to offer at each level, and how to promote memberships without alienating non-paying viewers
-  Brand deal pipeline: How to proactively reach 20 relevant brands per month using a cold email template that gets responses from even small channels
-  Revenue diversification target: For a channel my size, what the ideal revenue split is across AdSense, sponsorships, affiliates, and owned products
-  12-month income projection: Realistic monthly income estimates at 1K, 5K, 10K, 50K, and 100K subscribers based on my niche's CPM and audience demographics

Format as a monetization roadmap with income estimates by stage, activation sequence, and brand outreach templates.

My niche: [CHANNEL TOPIC]
Current subscriber count: [WHERE YOU ARE NOW]
Primary monetization goal: [ADSENSE / SPONSORSHIPS / COURSES / ALL OF THE ABOVE]

cases.content:
YouTube took down 2 of my recent videos, one was a cinematic teaser with nothing in DeFi being covered, simply a teaser.

The second explained how @revshare_app works, and how people could earn passive income from it.

The videos were removed for "Dangerous content", the channel received a strike and I was banned for 7 days.

I don't believe my content is dangerous, do I cover some risky DeFI sometimes? Yes. Do I clickbait? Yes, but then I deliver.

How do you peeps feel about the current state of Crypto YouTube?

cases.title:
During my research before I started  youtube channel, I came to learn that you can register from anywhere in the world bt what matters is your Audience is from which countries.

 If your content most
cases.content:
During my research before I started  youtube channel, I came to learn that you can register from anywhere in the world bt what matters is your Audience is from which countries.

 If your content most attracts Americans and European countries then your likely to earn more than those Audience from Africa and middle east.
You can open from USA bt your audience are Africans that's likely for you to earn low.

For instance i target audience from US and Europe, even posting your content timing is very important if you are targeting USA so that your content wont pop up in the middle of the night in US when everyone is asleep.

cases.title:
I was farming airdrops and writing about it in my Telegram channel at the same time. 

 Those were my 2 sources of income for a long period of time.  

And here's what I'll tell you:  I think we also
cases.content:
I was farming airdrops and writing about it in my Telegram channel at the same time. 

 Those were my 2 sources of income for a long period of time.  

And here's what I'll tell you:  I think we also have a hierarchy - everything you listed has nothing to do with me.

 I know how blockchain works and how Ethos works. Yes, I saw a video on YouTube by @0xCragHack . 

But I drew my own conclusions, did additional research, and formed my own opinion about this project.  

No drops = no new people in crypto period. 

Because then nobody needs it. All active users come after big drops on FOMO.  Some of them drop off, some stay. There's no other way. Everyone comes here to make money. Not to learn technology. 
There's no perspective here and nobody needs it if there's no money in it.  

Look at the AI sector. There's no money there - but everyone writes about it and goes there because there's a perspective. There are no drops but there's a future profession, something you can learn and earn from in the future.  

If you build crypto without drops - it won't be crypto. It'll be 2-3 projects that people use, and nothing else. 
My question is not even to the users but to the projects themselves - what are they building, what are they doing and how are they scamming people.   

Of course users and projects build each other. Those are the principles and rules here. 

 I actively farmed @SentientAGI - made 150 posts, a ton of events and creative work. I even sent you an access key.   I literally worked as an ambassador. Got $7000 in airdrop.  Dumped everything on day one.

  And what? 2 months have passed. I'm still writing about Sentient, interested in their technology.  The token isn't dumping and isn't getting rugged.   

Everything has held at the same level since TGE. All the believers hit the order book. But oh my god, it has zero effect on the order book.   
HOW IS THAT???   The token doesn't dump? Maybe because the team is solid? They built a community, connections, and positioned themselves in a certain way. 

 Now take 95% of projects as a comparison. Who didn't fulfill their obligations to people. Sometimes they just rugged.  

All your conclusions about the absence of airdrops being bad are wrong.  Conclusion 1. You need to be honest with each other. 

Like @megaeth . Promised nothing, gave nothing, nobody is upset. Although they've dragged out the mainnet. Nobody cares.  

Learn from @jessepollak - he says we'll turn airdrop hunters and multi-accounters into builders. 

And you know what, I became a builder thanks to you, Ethos and Base - I built https://t.co/caOUZaMeqB and I'm developing it.  

And where did I start? 
I came to tap @notcoin like an idiot to get an AIRDROP.That's where I started. 

Now think again whether airdrops are needed.

cases.title:
if i had to help @AlessandroLavis build a $100k/month info business on X - here's how i'd do it

and this is. the exact execution plan i'd implement to turn his AI TikTok expertise into a printing pre
cases.content:
if i had to help @AlessandroLavis build a $100k/month info business on X - here's how i'd do it

and this is. the exact execution plan i'd implement to turn his AI TikTok expertise into a printing press

let me break it down

CURRENT STATE ANALYSIS

alessandro's got solid pieces already in place

15k followers on X

positions himself as "the king of AI on TikTok"

teaches AI avatar systems, faceless content, TikTok shop affiliate pages

content shows $20k-$100k/month results from AI-powered pages

uses the classic "RT + comment [word] for the breakdown" engagement loop

the knowledge is clearly there

the audience is growing

but here's the problem

he's sitting on a goldmine and mining it with a fucking spoon

most people in his position make the same mistake

they build audience but don't build SYSTEMS that convert attention into cash

so they stay stuck doing engagement farming hoping it eventually turns into money

it won't

not without architecture

here's exactly what i'd build

PHASE 1: POSITIONING SHARPENING (week 1)

"the king of AI on TikTok" is decent but it's broad

who specifically is he helping?

- people who want to build AI faceless pages?
- agency owners adding AI content services?
- affiliates wanting automated traffic?
- complete beginners who want passive income?

each of these requires different messaging, different offers, different price points

i'd pick ONE primary audience and build the entire business around them

my recommendation: focus on the AFFILIATE angle

why?

because "$50k-$100k/month AI TikTok shop affiliate pages" is an insanely compelling promise

it's specific, it's measurable, it's desirable

and it attracts people with MONEY (affiliates already understand paying for knowledge that generates ROI)

the new positioning:

"i help affiliates build AI-powered TikTok pages that generate $50k+/month without filming, employees, or showing your face"

that's not "AI content creator"

that's a fucking money printing machine

and money printing machines sell themselves

PHASE 2: OFFER ARCHITECTURE (week 1-2)

right now alessandro's giving away breakdowns for free in DMs

that's fine for building audience but it's leaving money on the fucking table

here's the offer stack i'd build:

TIER 1: ENTRY PRODUCT ($47-$97)

"the AI affiliate page blueprint"

- exact page structure for high-converting AI tiktok pages
- avatar selection and cloning walkthrough
- script templates that drive shop sales
- the wan 2.2 workflow for face swapping

this captures people who aren't ready for high-ticket

and more importantly - it creates BUYERS

a buyer at $47 is 10x more likely to buy at $2,000 than a follower who's never paid you

TIER 2: CORE SYSTEM ($497-$997)

"AI affiliate empire"

the complete system for building and scaling faceless AI tiktok shop pages

- full course on the entire methodology
- template library (scripts, hooks, avatar sources)
- tool stack and setup tutorials
- case studies of winning pages
- private community access

this is the main revenue driver

most of his audience will buy here

TIER 3: IMPLEMENTATION ($3,000-$5,000)

"AI empire accelerator"

- 4-6 weeks of direct implementation support
- 1:1 or small group calls
- page audits and feedback
- direct telegram access to alessandro
- done-with-you setup of first AI page
- accountability and optimization

for people who want speed and hand-holding

this is where the real money is

one $5k client = 50 entry product sales

TIER 4: BACKEND ($10,000+)

"AI affiliate agency partnership" or done-for-you services

alessandro or his team builds their AI pages for them

or licenses his system to agencies

this is the long-term scale play

most people won't buy this at first

but after they see results from tier 2 and 3, they'll ask for it

PHASE 3: CONTENT SYSTEM OVERHAUL (week 2-3)

alessandro's content is good but it's all ATTRACTION

"look at this cool thing AI can do" 
"here's how these pages make money" 
"comment X and i'll send the breakdown"

that builds followers

but it doesn't build BUYERS

here's the content system i'd install:

BUCKET 1: PROOF CONTENT (40%)

this is what he's already doing but needs to amplify

screenshots of page earnings

specific numbers and metrics

before/after transformations

"i just built this page in 3 hours and it made $X"
but add a layer:

every proof post should hint at the SYSTEM behind it

"this page made $23k last month using my 4-step avatar cloning process"

now they want the process, not just the inspiration

BUCKET 2: FRAMEWORK CONTENT (35%)

this is where most info product creators fail

they either give away everything (no reason to buy) or nothing (no credibility)

the solution: give the WHAT and the WHY, sell the HOW

example:

"3 reasons most AI tiktok pages fail:

wrong product selection (they pick trending not converting)

weak hook structure (first 0.5 seconds don't stop the scroll)

inconsistent posting (algorithm buries you)
fix these three and you'll hit $10k/month minimum
i break down exactly how in [offer]"

they understand the problem

they see you know the solution

they need to pay to get the implementation

BUCKET 3: POLARIZING CONTENT (25%)

this builds cult-like following and filters tire-kickers

"most AI content courses are garbage - they teach you the tools not the SYSTEM"

"you don't need 100k followers to make $50k/month - you need ONE winning page structure"

"everyone's chasing viral when they should be chasing CONVERSIONS"

takes strong stances

attacks common beliefs

positions alessandro as different from everyone else

PHASE 4: DM CLOSING SYSTEM (week 3-4)

the "comment X and i'll DM you" strategy is solid for building audience

but it's incomplete

right now the flow is:

comment → DM breakdown → ???

there's no conversion system

here's what i'd build:

STEP 1: INBOUND TRIGGER (already happening)

"RT + comment 'AVATAR' for the full breakdown"

STEP 2: AUTOMATED QUALIFICATION

first DM back:

"yo just sent the breakdown - quick question, are you already doing TikTok affiliate or looking to start your first page?"

this tells you:

if they're a complete beginner (lower ticket)

if they're experienced (higher ticket potential)

if they're serious or just collecting free shit

STEP 3: VALUE + BRIDGE

send the breakdown as promised

then add:

"this is the foundation but the real magic is in the implementation - most people fuck up [specific thing] when they try to do this themselves. want me to send the full system that fixes that?"

now you've delivered value AND opened a loop

STEP 4: OFFER PRESENTATION

send a google doc with:

- who this is for
- what they get
- results/proof from others
- pricing options
- how to pay
- not a sales call booking link

A GOOGLE DOC THAT CLOSES

i've seen people close $5k+ deals with a well-structured google doc

no calls needed

STEP 5: OBJECTION HANDLING + FOLLOW-UP

most people don't buy immediately

that's fine

build a follow-up sequence:

24 hours: "any questions about the system?"

48 hours: "saw you checked out the doc - what's holding you back?"

72 hours: "closing enrollment friday - wanted to make sure you didn't miss it if this was something you wanted"

this alone recovers 20-30% of deals that would have died

PHASE 5: SCALE ENGINES (week 5-8)

once the foundation is printing, we pour gas on it

ENGINE 1: YOUTUBE LONG-FORM

tiktok and X are great for reach

youtube is great for DEPTH

take the best performing threads and turn them into 10-15 minute tutorials

"how i built a $50k/month AI tiktok page in 7 days" 
"the complete AI avatar cloning tutorial (step-by-step)" 
"AI affiliate marketing: my exact system revealed"

youtube videos rank for years

one good video can drive leads forever

ENGINE 2: AFFILIATE ARMY

people who buy the course and get results become the best salespeople

build a referral program:

30-40% commission on sales

swipe copy for them to use

leaderboard with bonuses

one good affiliate can drive $10-$20k/month in sales

ENGINE 3: STRATEGIC PARTNERSHIPS

who else serves affiliates and TikTok creators?
affiliate networks

TikTok shop communities

other info product creators in adjacent spaces

reach out and propose:

- guest content swaps
- bundle deals
- joint workshops

one partnership can unlock an entire new audience overnight

ENGINE 4: PAID AMPLIFICATION

once organic is converting, amplify it

take best performing posts and put $50-$100/day behind them

not "running ads"

PROMOTING CONTENT THAT ALREADY WORKS

way lower risk, way higher ROI

THE $100K/MONTH MATH

let's run the numbers:

ENTRY PRODUCT ($67): 150 sales/month = $10,050

CORE SYSTEM ($697): 60 sales/month = $41,820

IMPLEMENTATION ($4,000): 8 clients/month = $32,000

BACKEND ($12,000): 2 clients/month = $24,000

TOTAL: $107,870/month

aggressive? yes

possible with this system? absolutely

the product knowledge is already there

the audience is already growing

the results are already proven

the only thing missing is the ARCHITECTURE

WHAT ALESSANDRO NEEDS TO DO RIGHT NOW

week 1:

nail the positioning (affiliate-focused, specific transformation)

build offer docs for all 4 tiers

restructure bio and pinned to reflect new positioning

week 2:

- create 20 posts following the new bucket system
- install first version of DM qualification sequence

week 3-4:

- start actively closing in DMs using the system
- collect testimonials from first buyers

week 5-8:
- scale what's working
- add youtube/partnerships/affiliates
- optimize and compound

8 weeks from now this could be a completely different business

not because of luck

because of SYSTEMS

the knowledge is the easy part

he already has that

the architecture is what separates $5k/month from $100k/month

and now he has the architecture

time to fuckin build

cases.title:
Short-form video is the highest-reach organic content format available to real estate agents right now.

It's also the most misused.

Most agents who try video for lead generation make the same mistak
cases.content:
Short-form video is the highest-reach organic content format available to real estate agents right now.

It's also the most misused.

Most agents who try video for lead generation make the same mistake: they create content for buyers. Home tours, neighborhood walkthroughs, "things to know before you buy" videos. All fine, but buyers are a smaller pool and a longer conversion cycle.

The agents getting serious ROI from short-form video are focused on a different audience: homeowners who are thinking about selling.

Here's how to build a video strategy that generates seller leads.

The content categories that attract sellers.

Market update videos. A 45 to 90 second video at the beginning of each month: "Here's what's happening in [neighborhood] right now. [X] homes sold last month. Average price was [Y]. Homes are sitting for [Z] days. If you've been curious about your value, here's what that means for your street." Homeowners watch this. Every single person who watches it to the end is telling you they care about local market conditions. That's your audience.

"Just sold" breakdowns. Don't just post a sold graphic. Record a 60-second video: "I just helped a client sell on [street] for [price]. Here's what made this one interesting and what it tells us about buyer demand in the area right now." Neighbors of that home will watch it. Other homeowners in your farm will watch it.

Seller prep tips. Short, specific, actionable: "The one thing that costs sellers the most money in their home sale (and how to fix it for free)." "What your agent should be doing in the first 7 days your home is on market." "Why your first week of showings is the most important." These pull in homeowners who are in research mode, the ones actively thinking about selling but not yet ready to raise their hand.

The platforms to prioritize.

Instagram Reels and YouTube Shorts for search and algorithmic reach. TikTok if you have the tolerance for it, but it's not required. The key is posting the same video to at least two platforms simultaneously. The work of filming once, distributed twice, dramatically improves your return on the time invested.

Facebook still matters for the 40 to 65 age demographic, which is often your most motivated seller group. If your farm skews that direction, Facebook video is worth the extra upload.

The format that works without professional production.

You don't need a camera crew. You need:
- Good light (natural light facing you, not behind you)
- Clear audio (a $30 lapel mic makes more difference than any camera upgrade)
- A clear point in the first 3 seconds (your hook determines whether anyone watches past the opening)

The agents most self-conscious about being on video usually produce the most relatable content once they commit to it. Nobody is looking for a polished broadcaster. They're looking for a real agent who knows what they're talking about.

The call to action that converts viewers into leads.

End every seller-focused video the same way: "If you're curious what your home might be worth in this market, I do free no-obligation valuations for homeowners in [area]. Link in bio." Short, specific, no pressure.

The homeowner who watches your market update video three months in a row and then clicks that link is a warm lead. They chose to come to you. That's a very different conversation than a cold call.

What's your biggest hesitation about getting started with video? ↓

Follow @remarketingtips for weekly real estate marketing tactics.

cases.title:
ChatGPT and storytelling made me a millionaire at 22.

This edition of Creators Diary features Aliyah, a 23 year old content marketer and a graduate of Covenant University who earns about ₦3 million e
cases.content:
ChatGPT and storytelling made me a millionaire at 22.

This edition of Creators Diary features Aliyah, a 23 year old content marketer and a graduate of Covenant University who earns about ₦3 million every month. 

She currently has over 500k followers across all her social media platforms.

When I asked her how she makes her money, she said this:

“I already have everything planned out before coming online. I locked in for months taking relevant courses on storytelling, content marketing, writing, and selling. Thanks to platforms like hubspot and Coursera. 

I studied the top creators making big money online, and they all had one thing in common- they know how to tell stories.”

That was what inspired her.

She came online in early 2023. Back then, Instagram was the only account she had before expanding to other platforms. That was where everything started.

She even borrowed money from her uncle to buy a camera so she could record her videos.

Then she built her personal brand online using one thing - STORYTELLING.

She writes compelling articles and makes creative videos for brands. What she usually does is pick a growing brand and create dummy marketing videos for them. Most of the time, those videos go viral.

When these brands see the videos, they reach out, and most times, sign her instantly.

Her first big bag came when she made a video for a new clothing brand. The video went viral and made over ₦1 million in sales for the brand (they even posted it on their page). They ran ads for that same video, and got x5 of the previous sales.

She became their brand ambassador immediately. She also has five articles ranking on Google’s first page for her clients.

We talked about a lot of things, but here are the five things she kept repeating:

1. Storytelling will take you far.

If you want to grow faster online, tell stories. Whether it’s good or bad, make people feel something.
It’s the fastest growth hack online.
And not just any story, tell relatable stories, stories that make people take action.

2. Focus on one platform before spreading your wings.

But if you’re going to be on multiple platforms, be strategic.

She said,

“When I first started out, I tried to be on all platforms, but I always got burnt out. So I switched to repurposing. I’d turn my Instagram videos into long-form articles with AI tools.”

She added,

“ChatGPT is my favourite tool. It has made me a millionaire. It changed my career.”

3. Start selling something early.

She said,

“Sales isn’t about followers. I made my first ₦100k from selling an ebook when I had less than 5k followers.”

You don’t need a crowd before you sell. You need a product and a plan.

4. Run ads for your best-performing posts.

If a post already has high engagement and impressions - promote it.
That’s how you turn reach into revenue.

5. Video content is king.

Use YouTube, Instagram, and TikTok.
You can grow faster with video because people connect better with faces and stories.
Even your YouTube videos can rank on Google if optimized properly.

If you want to get featured on creators diary. I'm just a DM away.

(Recap)

cases.title:
the "ex-girlfriend method" made our fitness equipment client $1.845M from $5K/month in content.

simple concept: be everywhere they look. be impossible to forget.

here's why this works: 

➡️only 17%
cases.content:
the "ex-girlfriend method" made our fitness equipment client $1.845M from $5K/month in content.

simple concept: be everywhere they look. be impossible to forget.

here's why this works: 

➡️only 17% of buyers see an ad and purchase directly. the other 83% need multiple touchpoints before they commit.

➡️they see your ad. get curious. then the research spiral starts.

Google. YouTube. Reddit. Instagram. podcasts. news sites. ChatGPT. they're checking all of it before they pull out their card. and over 70% of this research happens outside Google entirely.

if you only show up in paid ads, you're invisible during this entire phase. your ad spend just funded your competitor's sale.

our client sells fitness equipment. treadmills, ellipticals, the same category as Peloton and NordicTrack.

they couldn't outspend those brands. Peloton and NordicTrack burn $100M+ annually on ads.

so they went the other direction.

we identified hundreds of questions their buyers ask before purchasing. 

everything from early research like "elliptical vs treadmill" to ready-to-buy searches like "Sole F-51 price."

then we created answers in every format. articles, videos, podcasts, infographics, social posts, reels, slideshows. 

distributed across 300+ platforms including YouTube, Spotify, Google News, FOX affiliates, Instagram, TikTok.

within 3 months: 11,475 monthly blog visitors. $258K in sales from organic traffic alone.

total result: $1.845 million from $5K/month in content. 61.5x return.

now they outrank Peloton and NordicTrack on buyer keywords.

cases.title:
MrBeast just told you why every other social platform is cooked.

$100B to creators over 4 years. TikTok’s original Creator Fund was $1B total. Instagram has been running abandoned experiments for a d
cases.content:
MrBeast just told you why every other social platform is cooked.

$100B to creators over 4 years. TikTok’s original Creator Fund was $1B total. Instagram has been running abandoned experiments for a decade. Snapchat Spotlight burned $250M in 2021 and gutted payouts within months.

YouTube pays 20-50x more per view than any competitor.

A finance creator gets $15-30 CPM on YouTube. That same creator gets $0.50-$2 on TikTok for identical content. The gap is so wide that serious creators treat every other platform as a marketing channel for their YouTube.

This changes what gets made.

When you can earn $50K/month from 2M views, you hire editors. You buy better cameras. You spend three weeks on research. You build sets. The production quality gap between YouTube and TikTok grows every year because the economics justify investment on one platform and not the other.

MrBeast himself is proof. He reinvests millions per video because YouTube’s economics make it rational. No TikTok-first creator can justify that spend.

The flywheel compounds. Higher payouts attract better creators. Better creators attract more viewers. More viewers attract higher ad rates. Higher ad rates fund higher payouts. YouTube has been running this loop for 15 years while competitors keep launching $200M creator funds and wondering why nobody treats them seriously.

$100B bought YouTube something no competitor can purchase: the entire professional creator class building their businesses on its infrastructure.

Every other platform is fighting for the scraps.
cases.author: aakashgupta

cases.content:
i started this online money thing when i was 14.

my first ever dollar on the internet came from designing posts on fiverr.
i made $15.
not per hour.
total.

i switched to video editing because designing was going nowhere for me.
spent months learning, trying, failing.
my first editing client paid me $3 per reel lol.
worked with him for a bit. then it stopped.

end of 2023, i landed another client. @orbed_ .
he paid $60 upfront for some tiktok edits. (still working with him till this day)
that $60 was a genuine turning point in my life.
i still remember how it felt.

not because it was a lot for me back in the day.
but because it proved something:
“oh shit… i can actually make real money on the internet.”

that payment gave me an insane boost.
i went harder. made good money with nick.
upgraded my entire setup thanks to him.

after some time i met a friend on discord and we started an editing agency together.
it completely flopped lol.
no clients. nothing.

at that time i was still making a couple thousand per month editing solo.
early 2025, we shut that agency down.
problems with the friend. lessons learned.

so i decided to started posting consistently on twitter as a video editor.
signed some clients here and there.
tested instagram content. tested outreach. learned a lot.
then in april 2025, i had a realization:

there is no way i scale to $10k, $20k, $50k+ per month as an editor.
it’s just a 9-5 from home lol.

still trading time for money.
still capped.
no leverage.
so i switched.

i went all in on building a content agency.
binged @/theaymanarab  youtube videos on how to start and scale an agency all day.
marketing, outreach, sales, youtube.

built the foundation in 2 days.
started outreach immediately.
it went… bad.
250+ outreaches.
1 call booked.
0 closed.

but that failure slapped me in the face in a good way.

“you’re ass at sales.”
“you’re ass at marketing.”
“you’re ass at outreach.”
so i doubled down on learning.

changed my outreach strategy.
started posting on youtube + instagram (inconsistent at first, got better)

turned 16yo in june 2025, and i landed my first dfy client.
$1.5k/month retainer.

that’s when things clicked.

i doubled down even harder.
more outreach. more replies. more learning.

ups and downs.
then september 2025 came.
fucking school started.

i went there for one month.
hated every second of it.

so i made a hard decision:
i dropped out of high school to go all in on my business.
it wasn't easy
a lot of arguments with my parents at the beginning.

but after that they were okay with it because i already proved i could make money.

first month after dropping out: $10k.
next month: $7k.
life got too comfortable in my hometown in morocco.
so i decided to leave.
booked a flight to malaysia and bali with @AhmedElsaleet & @CzechMarquise .
locked in together in one apartment.

traveling showed me how many opportunities exist.
how much bigger the world is.
how much more is possible.

now i’m focused on:

scaling

building my personal brand
building a strong team
signing more clients
getting insane results for clients
goal for 2026: $100k/month.

i have 0% regret dropping out of high school.
literally none.

take the risk.
but make sure you’re ready to do everything for it.

cases.title:
I cracked the laziest way to print money online without showing your face.

sounds like bait. it's not.

I run faceless accounts that make $4-8K monthly each.

I don't reply to comments. barely check
cases.content:
I cracked the laziest way to print money online without showing your face.

sounds like bait. it's not.

I run faceless accounts that make $4-8K monthly each.

I don't reply to comments. barely check DMs. post from my bed in boxers.

one account made $2,400 last month and I think I spent maybe 3 hours on it total.

let me break down exactly how this works.

THE FACELESS MODEL

no selfies. no camera. no "personal brand."

just valuable content and a product link.

people find the content. get value. click the profile. see the offer. buy.

I'm not in the equation at all.

the account is the asset. not me.

which means I can run multiple. sell them whenever. stay completely anonymous.

nobody knows these accounts are mine. not friends. not family. nobody.

just money appearing from profiles that don't have my name attached.

WHY THIS IS THE LAZIEST VERSION

youtube: film for 4 hours, edit for 8 hours, thumbnail for 2 hours, pray it performs

tiktok: dance like an idiot, post 5x daily, content dies in 24 hours, start over forever

instagram: photoshoots, aesthetic maintenance, reels editing, story updates constantly

faceless X: type some words, schedule them, collect money

I batch 20-25 tweets on sunday morning while watching youtube.

takes maybe 2 hours.

schedule everything. don't touch the account until next sunday.

sales notifications pop up throughout the week while I do whatever I want.

that's the whole "job."

THE PRODUCT SIDE

I sell info products. digital files.

PDFs. templates. script packs. guides.

create once. sells forever.

my laziest product took an afternoon to make.

it's done over $11K.

same file. just sitting there. generating income month after month.

no fulfillment. no customer service. no calls.

someone buys at 3am in singapore. they get instant access. I wake up to money.

94% profit margins because there's nothing to pay for.

THE NICHES THAT WORK FACELESS

not everything works without a face.

these do:

AI ONLYFANS

teaching people to run OF accounts using AI-generated models.

no real person needed. pure AI.

people making $20-50K monthly doing this.

teaching the skill is even more profitable.

this market barely existed 18 months ago. ground floor stuff.

AI UGC

brands desperate for AI-generated content.

creators charging $5-15K monthly.

selling templates and tutorials about it? basically no competition.

HIGH TICKET SALES

closers pulling $10-30K monthly.

scripts. frameworks. call breakdowns.

salespeople always invest in getting better.

NICHE TEMPLATES

notion for real estate agents.
spreadsheets for amazon sellers.
canva for course creators.

specific + boring = money.

the less sexy it sounds, the less competition you have.

HOW I SET ONE UP

WEEKEND 1: THE PRODUCT

saturday morning: brain dump everything I know about solving one specific problem

saturday afternoon: organize it into sections, write it out conversationally

sunday: design in canva, make it look clean, upload to gumroad

done. product exists.

WEEKEND 2: THE ACCOUNT

create anonymous profile. logo or abstract image for the pic.

bio that says exactly what value the account provides.

link straight to product.

write 30 tweets about the problem the product solves.

schedule them across 10 days.

account is live and running.

EVERY WEEK AFTER

sunday: batch 20-25 tweets

monday: schedule everything

tuesday-saturday: literally nothing

check sales whenever I feel like it. usually don't.

THE CONTENT APPROACH

faceless means you can't rely on personality.

pure value carries the account.

what works:

how-to breakdowns
common mistakes people make
quick tips they can use immediately
frameworks they can steal
results and proof

what doesn't work:

"day in my life" content
personal stories about your childhood
hot takes that require knowing who you are
anything needing your face or voice

education is the play. become the account that teaches [specific thing] better than anyone.

THE NUMBERS BREAKDOWN

let me show you the math on one account.

followers: 4,200
product price: $34
monthly sales: roughly 70
monthly revenue: $2,380

time invested weekly: 2-3 hours

that's like $200/hour if you do the math.

and it compounds. more followers = more sales = same time investment.

the account from 6 months ago making $800/month now makes $2,400/month.

I didn't work harder. just kept the system running.

SCALING THE LAZY WAY

one account doing $3K/month is cool.

three accounts doing $3K each is $9K/month.

same strategy. different niches.

I copy the exact system to a new niche. set it up over a weekend or two. let it run.

some accounts do better than others.

I keep the winners. kill the losers. add new ones.

portfolio approach.

right now I run several that combine for $18K+ monthly.

total time across all of them: maybe 8-10 hours per week.

most of that is the sunday batching sessions.

THE SELLABLE ASSET ANGLE

here's what most people miss.

faceless accounts aren't just income streams.

they're sellable assets.

standard valuation: 30-40x monthly revenue.

account doing $5K/month = $150-200K sale price.

or just keep it and collect monthly.

either way you're building equity. not just income.

personal brands can't do this. nobody's buying "you."

faceless accounts are pure business assets.

WHY MOST PEOPLE WON'T DO THIS

it sounds too simple.

there must be a catch. some hidden complexity I'm not mentioning.

there isn't.

the catch is that simple doesn't mean you'll actually execute.

you still need to:

pick a niche and commit
create something worth buying
show up consistently for months
not quit when month 2 feels pointless

most people can't do boring and consistent.

they want exciting and complicated.

so they never start. or they start and stop within 3 weeks.

which is perfect for the people who actually stick with it.

less competition.

THE TIMELINE

month 1-2: setting up, barely any sales, questioning everything
month 3-4: traction starting, first consistent sales
month 5-6: momentum building, systems clicking
month 7-12: real income, $3-8K monthly per account

per account.

multiply by however many you want to run.

THE LAZY LIFESTYLE

I wake up when I wake up.

no alarm. no commute. no boss.

sunday morning I batch content while drinking coffee.

rest of the week I do whatever.

sales notifications come in randomly throughout the day.

money from accounts nobody knows I own.

complete anonymity. complete freedom.

that's what this model gives you.

not fame. not followers who recognize you.

just income and time.

WHO THIS IS FOR

people who want money without attention.

people with jobs who can't show their face online.

introverts who think "personal brand" sounds exhausting.

people who want sellable assets, not personality-dependent income.

anyone who looked at youtubers filming for 10 hours and thought "absolutely not."

this is the lazy path. the anonymous path. the path nobody knows you're on.

WHO THIS ISN'T FOR

if you want to be famous, this isn't it.

nobody will recognize you. no clout. no "influencer" status.

you'll just have money coming in from accounts nobody knows are yours.

for some people that's a downside.

for me it's the whole point.

THE COMPLETE SYSTEM

I documented everything.

how to pick niches that actually work faceless.
creating products in a weekend.
content strategy that sells without engagement.
the batching system I use.
scaling to multiple accounts.
real numbers from accounts I run.

the whole thing. nothing held back.

$50.

what one of these accounts makes in like half a day.

your first sale covers it. everything after is pure profit.

comment "FACELESS" and I'll DM you the link.

follow and RT first so I can actually send it.

or keep thinking this sounds too easy while accounts like mine collect money in the background.

they're out there printing whether you believe it or not.

might as well be yours.

cases.title:
8 months ago I made a TikTok about an app idea as a joke.

It went viral and got 150K views.

4 days later the app was on the App Store.

The app: Goon Tracker.

Global leaderboard. Clans. Friends lis
cases.content:
8 months ago I made a TikTok about an app idea as a joke.

It went viral and got 150K views.

4 days later the app was on the App Store.

The app: Goon Tracker.

Global leaderboard. Clans. Friends list. Compete against your boys to see who gooned the most.

Genuinely the dumbest idea I've ever had.

I built it because I thought it was funny. No market research. No competitor analysis. No "validation phase." No notion board with color-coded categories.

Just vibes.

7 days after launch: first in-app purchase came through.

I remember staring at my phone like "wait, someone actually paid for this?"

Then it happened again. And again.

Here's where it gets stupid.

I have barely touched the app since launch.

No updates. No bug fixes. No new features. No marketing. No paid ads. No influencer outreach. No App Store optimization guides. Nothing.

8 months of doing absolutely nothing.

December revenue: $150.

Pure organic. Pure passive. Pure "I forgot this app existed" energy.

$150 isn't life-changing money. But that's not the point.

The point is I spent less time building this app than you spend 'researching' every week.

Meanwhile you're on month 6 of "validating your idea."

You've watched 47 YouTube videos about App Store Optimization. You've joined 3 Discord servers about indie development. You've read every single tweet from developers making $50K/month and save their threads to "study later."

You've done everything except actually ship something.

(these are grown adults btw)

You know what the difference is between me and you?

I posted a stupid TikTok about a stupid idea and mass-produced the stupid app in 4 days.

You're still trying to figure out if your idea is "good enough."

Spoiler: it's not. None of them are. Ship it anyway.

The dumber your app idea, the more money it makes.

I'm not even joking. The apps that make the most money are embarrassingly simple. Fart soundboards. Fasting timers. Habit trackers that do one thing.

You're out here trying to build the next Notion and wondering why you've been "working on it" for 9 months with nothing to show.

Your mass-produced calorie counter would've made more than your "revolutionary productivity system" ever will.

But you won't build it because it feels beneath you.

So you'll keep researching. Keep validating. Keep saving threads. Keep telling yourself you're being strategic.

And 12 months from now you'll still be exactly where you are today.

Except now you'll be mass-producing excuses about why the market was too saturated or why your timing was wrong.

The market isn't saturated. Your execution is just mid.

I made an app about tracking who gooned the most.

It makes money while I sleep.

What's your excuse?

cases.title:
I used to work at a coffee shop making less than minimum wage.

One of my co-workers was a guy named Mason.

We’d talk shit, make lattes, clock out, repeat

Eventually we both quit.

That was 2018.

F
cases.content:
I used to work at a coffee shop making less than minimum wage.

One of my co-workers was a guy named Mason.

We’d talk shit, make lattes, clock out, repeat

Eventually we both quit.

That was 2018.

Fast forward to 2022.

I’m scrolling TikTok and my old colleague @CardinalMason pops up on my For You page

Except now he's "Cardinal Mason" talking about making $40-50K a month from copywriting.

I'm sitting there as a broke college student:

“damn I used to work with this guy”

We used to make the same minimum wage.

Now he's pulling in more per month than I made all year.

So, like many of us did after seeing Mason on their FYP.

I started copywriting.

Joined his and @yourealazyfvck program for $20 a week.

Started cold calling businesses every day.

(I was getting destroyed)

Business owners telling me "how did you get my number" and "why the f*ck are you calling me."

But I had this funny idea:

I started recording and posting the rejections, hang-ups and awkward silences on TikTok.

To my surprise, people loved it.

Videos started hitting 50-100K views.

But after 6 months of learning, cold calling, and outreaching, I did the math:

I made $600.

Not $600 a week or $600 a day.

$600 in 6 months.

The content was popping off but my bank account didn't care.

Then one day I get a DM on Twitter from a guy named Omar.

He's running @HasbullaHive 1.5 million followers Twitter account.

He found me by searching "copywriting" on TikTok.

My cold call videos came up.

He wanted me to teach him.

I'm thinking:

"holy shit, this guy works with Hasbulla"

So I pitch him:

"Hasbulla has a clothing brand with zero email marketing. Let me run it. In exchange, I'll teach you everything I know."

He pitched it to Hasbulla's manager.

They said yes.

That was the first time Omar and I ever worked together.

Content opened that door.

An embarrassing TikTok video of me getting rejected.

We ran Hasbulla's email marketing for a few months until they had to shut down the store.

But then the manager hit us up.

"Would you guys want to just make content for us instead? Instagram, YouTube, whatever."

We said sure.

And something clicked.

Making content was way more fun than writing emails.

We were actually good at it.

So we started a content agency.

Our first big client after Hasbulla was BucketSquad, a YouTube channel with 20 million subscribers.

We were literally getting paid thousands of dollars a month to make memes.

From there we niched down into short form, then long form YouTube.

Started working with athletes like Tracy McGrady, Danny Green, and Takeo Spikes.

(As well as a few others I can't name)

Branched to working with coaches, agency owners, and B2B founders.

Since then we've done over $2M in revenue for our clients through YouTube, this year alone.

All organic.

Three years ago I was getting cursed out on the phone by business owners who didn't want to hear from me.

Now those same types of business owners pay us to build their YouTube channels.

Weird how things work out.

But forever grateful for trusting in the process.

cases.title:
how manus + instagram reels hit $18k/m AI info in 20 days

while everyone's grinding on twitter building text-based audiences slowly

instagram reels are completely UNTAPPED for info products

and man
cases.content:
how manus + instagram reels hit $18k/m AI info in 20 days

while everyone's grinding on twitter building text-based audiences slowly

instagram reels are completely UNTAPPED for info products

and manus makes exploiting them stupidly easy

here's the exact system that went from $0 to $18k/month in 20 days

THE INSTAGRAM REELS OPPORTUNITY

reels get 10-50x more reach than feed posts on instagram

algorithm is DESPERATE for good reels content

pushes your content to non-followers aggressively

one reel can reach 50k-500k people

even with 0 followers

twitter: need 5k followers before decent reach

instagram reels: can get 100k views with 200 followers

this is the arbitrage play bro

and 99% of info product sellers are sleeping on it

because they think "reels are for dancers and comedy"

wrong, reels are for WHOEVER understands the algorithm

WHY MANUS IS THE SECRET WEAPON

manus specializes in DATA ANALYSIS at massive scale

it can analyze thousands of reels in minutes

extract patterns that work

identify winning formulas

tell you exactly what to create

here's what manus does that you can't:

analyzes 1000 top-performing reels in your niche

identifies common hooks, structures, lengths, music, captions

extracts winning formulas you can replicate

validates which topics have viral potential

finds gaps competitors aren't filling

this turns guessing into SCIENCE

THE 20-DAY BREAKDOWN

here's exactly what happened day by day:

DAY 1-3: NICHE SELECTION AND RESEARCH

used manus to analyze 5 potential niches:

"analyze top 500 instagram reels in these niches: productivity, side hustles, fitness, personal finance, dating advice, identify: which niche has highest engagement, which topics within niche perform best, what content gaps exist"

manus came back with data:

productivity reels: 2.4M avg views on top posts

side hustle reels: 3.8M avg views on top posts ← WINNER

fitness reels: 1.9M avg views

finance reels: 2.1M avg views

dating reels: 1.6M avg views

picked side hustles based on pure data

not passion, not gut feel, DATA

DAY 4-5: PATTERN EXTRACTION

used manus to extract winning formulas:

"analyze top 100 side hustle reels with 1M+ views, identify: common hook patterns, optimal video length, music choices, caption structures, CTA strategies, what makes them stop scroll"

manus revealed patterns:

best performing hooks: "I made $X doing Y (nobody talks about this)"

optimal length: 15-30 seconds (completion rate matters)

music: trending audio increases reach 3-4x

captions: first 5 words critical for hook, emoji usage increases engagement 27%

CTAs: "DM me [WORD]" converts better than "link in bio"

i now had FORMULA not guesswork

DAY 6-8: CONTENT PRODUCTION SPRINT

created 30 reels in 3 days using the formula

didn't show face (faceless works on reels too)

used screen recordings, b-roll, text overlays, stock footage

structure for every reel:

0-2 seconds: pattern-interrupting hook from manus data

"I make $8k/month doing this and you've never heard of it"

3-15 seconds: quick value bomb or framework

"here's the 3-step system"

16-25 seconds: proof or specific example

"made $2400 last week using this"

26-30 seconds: strong CTA

"DM me SYSTEM to get the complete guide"

used trending audio manus identified

batch-created all 30 reels in 8 hours using templates

DAY 9-10: PRODUCT CREATION

while reels were being made, built offer with claude:

$47 "Side Hustle Starter System"

5 proven side hustles you can start today

complete frameworks for each

income projection calculators

implementation checklists

created in 6 hours using claude

simple, entry-level, impulse-buy price point

set up gumroad page with payment link

DAY 11: LAUNCH

posted first 3 reels on day 11

used scheduling tool to post 3x daily going forward

every reel had same CTA: "DM me START for the system"

within 4 hours: first reel hit 12k views

within 8 hours: 47 DMs asking about system

within 24 hours: first 6 sales = $282

holy shit, this works

DAY 12-15: SCALING VOLUME

kept posting 3 reels per day

some duds (2k-5k views)

some hits (50k-200k views)

but EVERY reel drove DMs

even "failed" reels with 3k views generated 5-10 DMs

by day 15:

total views across reels: 840k

total DMs: 380

total sales: 68 = $3,196

$3k in 5 days from brand new account

DAY 16-20: UPSELL INTRODUCTION

this is where it got insane

everyone who bought $47 product got email:

"loved the starter system? here's the complete $497 implementation package"

used claude to write perfect ascension email

focused on: you got quick wins with starter, now go deeper with complete system

conversion rate: 22% of $47 buyers upgraded to $497

by day 20:

total $47 sales: 127 = $5,969

total $497 upgrades: 28 = $13,916

total revenue in 20 days: $19,885

rounded to $18k for conservative estimate in title

THE MANUS ADVANTAGE BREAKDOWN

manus did things impossible manually:

COMPETITIVE ANALYSIS AT SCALE

manually analyzing 100 reels: 10-15 hours

manus analyzing 1000 reels: 15 minutes

manus found patterns i would've missed:

"reels using specific trending audio get 4.2x more reach"

"hooks mentioning specific dollar amounts ($4,273 vs $4k) perform 31% better"

"text overlay with yellow background converts 19% better than white"

these micro-optimizations compound into massive edge

CONTINUOUS OPTIMIZATION

every 3 days i fed manus new data:

"analyze my last 9 reels, which performed best, what patterns should i double down on, what should i
Avoid"

manus told me exactly what to do more of:

"your reels showing specific income screenshots get 3x more DMs than framework-only reels, create more proof-heavy content"

i adjusted strategy immediately based on data

results improved every 3-day cycle

TREND IDENTIFICATION

manus monitored trending topics:

"what side hustle topics are trending this week on reels"

told me AI side hustles were spiking

i created 5 reels about AI side hustles

those 5 reels did 380k views combined

vs my average of 40k per reel

9.5x better performance from trend riding

THE INSTAGRAM DM SALES SYSTEM

here's how i handled 380 DMs in 20 days without burning out:

AUTOMATED FIRST RESPONSE

when someone DMs "START"

manychat sends automatic reply:

"here's what you get in the Side Hustle Starter System [bullet points], it's $47 and you get instant access, ready to start? reply YES"

this filters tire kickers immediately

50% reply YES, 50% ghost

QUALIFICATION IN DMS

for those who reply YES:

"quick question before i send link - are you looking to start side hustle in next 30 days or just researching?"

if they say "next 30 days" → QUALIFIED

if they say "just researching" → "cool, here's free guide to get started" + disengage

only spend time on BUYERS not browsers

INSTANT CLOSE

qualified buyers get immediately:

"perfect, here's the link [gumroad], once you're in check your email for access, let me know if any questions"

no back and forth, no "let me think about it"

direct, immediate, transaction-focused

70% of qualified leads buy within 5 minutes

THE CONTENT FORMULA THAT WORKED

every viral reel followed this structure:

HOOK (0-2 SEC): SPECIFIC + CONTRARIAN

bad: "want to make money online?"

good: "I make $8k/month selling notion templates (took 4 hours to set up)"

specificity + low-effort angle = stops scroll

VALUE (3-20 SEC): FRAMEWORK OR PROOF

show the system in simple terms

or show proof it works (screenshots, testimonials)

don't explain everything, TEASE the full system

CTA (21-30 SEC): DIRECT AND CLEAR

"DM me START to get the complete system"

not "link in bio" (people won't click)

not "comment below" (low commitment)

DM = direct conversation = high conversion

THE REELS CREATION WORKFLOW

you don't need fancy equipment or video skills

here's my actual process:

STEP 1: SCREEN RECORDING (10 MINUTES)

record phone screen showing:

income dashboard

product being sold

testimonial screenshots

framework diagram

anything that proves concept works

STEP 2: B-ROLL FOOTAGE (FREE)

download free b-roll from pexels

aesthetic shots that match niche

workspace, laptop, coffee, etc.

creates visual interest

STEP 3: CAPCUT EDITING (15 MINUTES)

import screen recording + b-roll

add text overlays with hooks and value points

add trending audio (manus tells you which)

add captions (auto-generated)

render and export

STEP 4: CAPTION WITH HOOK

first line of caption = hook from video

next 2-3 lines = expand on value

final line = CTA with emojis

hashtags: mix of big and niche (manus identifies best ones)

entire reel created in 25-30 minutes

batch 10 reels in one 5-hour session

THE MANUS RESEARCH PROMPTS

exact prompts i used with manus:

INITIAL ANALYSIS:

"analyze top 500 reels in [niche] hashtag from last 30 days, identify: average views, engagement rate, common hooks, optimal video length, trending audio used, caption patterns, what separates top 10% from rest"

COMPETITOR DEEP DIVE:

"analyze these 10 accounts [list competitors], what content performs best for each, what gaps exist in their content strategy, what topics are they not covering that have search demand"

CONTINUOUS OPTIMIZATION:

"analyze my last 20 reels [provide data], which performed best and why, what patterns should i double down on, what's not working that i should stop, what new angles should i test based on current trends"

TREND MONITORING:

"what topics in [niche] are getting most traction on reels this week, are there emerging trends i should capitalize on, what audio tracks are trending that i should use"

THE MATH BEHIND $18K

here's the actual funnel breakdown:

total reach: 840k views across 30 reels

DM rate: 0.45% (380 DMs from 840k views)

qualification rate: 50% (190 qualified from 380 DMs)

$47 conversion: 67% (127 sales from 190 qualified)

$497 upsell conversion: 22% (28 upgrades from 127 buyers)

revenue breakdown:

$47 product: 127 × $47 = $5,969

$497 product: 28 × $497 = $13,916

total: $19,885 in 20 days

CAC: $0 (organic reels)

profit margin: 97% (only costs were tools)

this is REAL BUSINESS not hype

WHY REELS BEAT OTHER PLATFORMS FOR SPEED

twitter: need to build following first, takes months

reels: instant distribution to non-followers

youtube: need watch time and subs for algorithm

reels: algorithm pushes you immediately

tiktok: works similarly but harder to drive to external offers

reels: can DM directly on platform, easier conversion

linkedin: slow engagement, corporate audience

reels: fast engagement, entrepreneurial audience

pinterest: good for passive but slow

reels: explosive reach if you hit formula

reels give you SPEED TO REVENUE

cases.content:
When we hit 10K organic views per day our ad costs dropped because people were already warm

Made $1K/day purely from organic traffic that never even saw an ad
One main account posting 5-10x/day (video ads -> organic)

https://t.co/1KVpKbusQ3 pushes every post to 25 other accounts automatically

Each account runs through its own proxy via AdsPower so platforms don't flag you

Math: 1 post → 25 accounts → minimum 2,500 views if each does 100 views
That's 2,500 free brand impressions per post
5 posts/day = 12,500 impressions daily for the cost of creating 5 pieces of content

Your competitor is paying $6,250 for those same impressions at $0.50 CPM

- It's not just about the organic sales
- It's that your entire funnel performs better
- Someone sees your TikTok, doesn't buy
- Sees your IG reel from account 3 later, doesn't buy
- Sees your Pinterest pin, doesn't buy
- Then your Facebook ad hits them and they convert

Without the organic layer you're trying to cold convert
With it you're closing warm traffic.

Your ROAS goes up, your CPA goes down, and you're getting direct organic revenue on top.

It shit costs $70/month to run:

- MyPrivateProxy for the proxies
- AdsPower to manage accounts
- https://t.co/1KVpKbusQ3 to auto-distribute
- If needed a VA who posts on the main platform daily

Started with 5 accounts per platform.
Now running 25 total across Instagram, TikTok, YouTube, Pinterest, X.
Got a VA managing comments via CommentGuard so the AI handles replies.

Everyone's ignoring organic because it seems slow and unsexy.

Meanwhile it's the leverage that makes your paid ads actually work

You're paying for awareness when you could be getting it free

And the direct organic revenue is just bonus money at 90%+ margins
The brands winning aren't choosing organic OR paid
They're using organic to warm traffic so paid converts better

Set up the infrastructure once, costs less than one day of ad spend, runs forever

If you want me to DM you the actual tools and loom of me setting this up like, repost, and comment “organic” (MUST BE FOLLOWING TO RECEIVE DM)

cases.title:
The genius marketing strategy that made Andrew Tate blow up

Many say he “invented clipping”

By mid-2022, Tate was making ~$10M per month

This is how:
- The viral engine was his course Hustler’s Uni
cases.content:
The genius marketing strategy that made Andrew Tate blow up

Many say he “invented clipping”

By mid-2022, Tate was making ~$10M per month

This is how:
- The viral engine was his course Hustler’s University (later rebranded as The Real World), which cost $49–$99 monthly
- Members earned commissions (up to 50% of referrals) by posting Tate’s clips on TikTok, YouTube + Instagram, embedding affiliate links
- This created a vast network of fan accounts which flooded platforms with his content
- Tate explicitly encouraged inflammatory content to maximise engagement, as he knew algorithms reward controversy
- The more users react, the more they comment + this pushes clips further into algo creating virality 
- Clippers also made money from ad revenue + at some point it was almost guaranteed that posting clips of Tate would get you views, so there was a strong incentive to post them
- In 2021 (launch of his course) he was making $600K-$1M per month, by 2022 he was making up to $11M per month with ~221K subs at peak

Nowadays Tate’s private group The Real World attracts over 155K subs, generating ~$5M profit monthly after paying his affiliates

Revenue was steady over the years, despite being affected by platform-wide bans including Instagram, Facebook, TikTok, YouTube + even Stripe

This is a lesson on the power of building a cult-like community + aligned incentives

(Note: I don’t like the guy, but we can respect strategy)

just text on screen with a voiceover explaining stuff people are embarrassed to ask about

here's why this works better than anything "creative"

viral content attracts everyone

and people with specific problems are buyers

a 24 year old watching dance trends has no money

a 38 year old watching "how to negotiate medical debt" has a problem they'll pay to solve

his slideshows get 50k views

the dance trends get 5M views

but his 50k views convert at 4% to his $297 course

do the math:

from slideshows about tax forms

the "boring" stuff is the stuff with commercial intent

nobody searches "how does a 401k match work" for entertainment

they search it because they have money and need answers

that's the audience you want

here's what he actually does

step 1: finds questions people are too embarrassed to ask

reddit threads like:

these get thousands of upvotes

canva slides with simple text AI voiceover reading the explanation stock background music upload to tiktok/reels/youtube shorts

zero daily effort after that

$297 for lifetime access

the course is just 30 more slideshow videos going deeper

still sells daily

people watching "how credit scores work" slideshows are:

they're pre-qualified buyers

you can get 10M views on a dance and make $0

home ownership basics

– refinancing explained

career/job stuff

– reading employment contracts 
– 401k matching explained 
– stock options breakdown

these are BORING

that's why they work

nobody's making entertaining content about this
because it's not sexy

but the people who need this info have MONEY

the content production system

batch content creation:

sunday (3 hours): 
– research 20 questions from reddit/quora 
– write scripts for each (5 min each) 
– create canva templates (reuse same design)

– render all 20 videos

monday-friday:

monetize it:

tier 1: free slideshows (top of funnel) → builds trust, attracts audience

tier 2: $97 complete guide → all topics in one place, templates included

tier 3: $297 course → deeper training, worksheets, email support

people start with free content

free content viewer → email subscriber (30%) subscriber → $97 buyer (3%) $97 buyer → $297 upsell (20%) $297 buyer → $997 session (5%)

now the math makes sense:

cases.title: We interviewed Ed Lawrence (Film Booth),  a YouTube millionaire with a 370K-sub track record, hundreds of millions of views, and one of the few creators who’s actually built multiple 7-figure business
cases.content:
We interviewed Ed Lawrence (Film Booth),  a YouTube millionaire with a 370K-sub track record, hundreds of millions of views, and one of the few creators who’s actually built multiple 7-figure businesses from YouTube.

He didn’t just “grow a channel.”
He rebuilt his entire system from scratch… and made 4× more revenue with fewer views.

The 12 lessons he shared will change how you look at YouTube forever 👇 

1/ Optimize for buyers, not viewers

Ed walked away from a massively successful channel — 370K subs, multi-million views, because it was optimized for views, not revenue or lifestyle.

Huge, broad, cinematic videos =
• massive effort
• production-company headaches
• little connection to what buyers actually want

On his new, niche channel, he rebuilt everything around one type of buyer and ended up 4×’ing revenue to ~$4M/yr.

Small channel. Bigger business.

2/ Pick ONE avatar & their top 3 problems

The old channel was talking to everyone:
• faceless creators
• educators
• entertainers
• hobbyists
• business owners

When you talk to everyone, nothing sticks, and nothing sells.

New strategy:
One buyer: business owners using YouTube to grow.
Three recurring problems: clarified through surveys, sales calls, and usage behavior.

Every video = solving one (or more) of those same 3 problems.

That consistency makes people buy.

3/ YouTube Studio is lying to you (a little)

He showed us two videos:

• 35K views → $90,000
• 300K views → $9,000

Guess which one YouTube Studio rewarded?

The wrong one.

YouTube said “10/10 don’t make this again.”
His business dashboard said “1/10, do this WEEKLY.”

The only Studio metric he actually cares about:
Returning viewers, the warm audience pool that drives buys.

4/ Warm audience depletion is real

Views can go up while revenue goes down.

Why?
Your warm audience (people who like you + might buy) shrinks if you don’t replenish it.

Fix:
• Speak to the same buyer every week
• Grow email like your life depends on it
• Use email to warm, nurture, and promote

Your warm audience is the engine. Everything else is decoration.

5/ CTR on your links = real leverage

Average creator: ~1% of viewers click links.
Ed: 8–10%.

His playbook:

• Mention CTA in first 90s (after value)
• Tie it directly to the problem the video just solved
• Simple, distraction-free landing page
• Mention it 2–3 times throughout

Cold viewers keep watching.
Warm viewers click.
Money follows warm viewers.

6/ The best CTA = tools you use inside the video

If you use a spreadsheet, calculator, template, or AI planner in the video…

…and then say “btw I made this, it’s free, link below,”

People naturally grab it.

Even without a verbal CTA,
“Free AI planner below”
keeps printing emails in the background.

7/ The About page link is a hidden money printer

Across half a billion tracked views, the About page link consistently ranks among the top 3 most-clicked links, often #1.

On his channel, it’s generated $500K+ since January alone.

Put your main offer there.
Not your IG.
Not your Twitter.
Not your random socials.

8/ Community posts + email = growth even without uploads

During a rebuild phase, Ed stopped uploading for 6 months…
and still grew the business using:

• 1 email per week
• 5 community posts per week

His community post formula:

Hook → problem → tease →
“I’ll email the full breakdown at 3pm, join the list.”

One good post = 800+ new email subs

tens of thousands in sales.

Imagine ignoring that because “community posts don’t matter.”

9/ End screens are your second algorithm

End screens are the only part of YouTube you fully control.

He sends every end screen to either:
• his highest-converting video, or
• the deepest video tied to the offer

End-screen CTR can hit ~20%,
vs 3% browse CTR.

That second video often leads straight to the email list → sale.

10/ Narrow, high-value offers > broad, cheap ones

Client example:

Went from a $78/mo product (~$800/year)
to $9K/year clients…

Hit $37K/month in 12 weeks, with the same views.

Another client in a tiny niche (permanent makeup / jewelry) got 300–400 views per video…
and still hit $100K/month.

Why?
High-value audience + aligned offer = disproportionate revenue.

11/ Cut distractions: YouTube + email is enough

Ed dropped:

Twitter
Instagram
Shorts
TikTok
Most experiments

He kept:
YouTube + email + occasional paid tests

More focus = more money.

12/ Think in decades, not videos

Each video is a test.
Expect a 50-video pain zone.
Most creators quit in the middle.

When something hits, don’t reinvent the wheel, reuse the structure, idea, and packaging.

Long-term thinking + consistent iteration = the only real growth hack. 

Follow for more creator growth insights :)

cases.title:
800 million views generated from going viral for myself and for companies making TikToks

Millions in revenue, millions of purchases, millions of followers

viral strategies cracked, 100+ creator team
cases.content:
800 million views generated from going viral for myself and for companies making TikToks

Millions in revenue, millions of purchases, millions of followers

viral strategies cracked, 100+ creator teams built

everything I’m about to share can be used for your app or your brand

DO WHATEVER YOU WANT WITH THIS INFO:

If your first hesitation for why you “shouldn’t” market on TikTok is because your “demographic” isn’t on there… put the fries in that bag.

TikTok is a daily search engine now. For damn near everybody.

It’s the modern-day short form YouTube because people got the attention span of a 4 y/o

Want to learn to drive a car in 30 seconds?

Search it on TikTok

Want to learn how to approach a girl?

Search it on TikTok

Your audience is on there. The second you use that excuse, you’re coping.

Market research is the only way you really know anything.

How do you market research?:

I got you. It’s stupid simple. In fact mf, after reading this tweet and you’re like “gee I got an idea,” here’s exactly what to do:

Go on TikTok and search whatever niche you want to build an app around

Let’s say you’re building an app for you and your friends so y’all can plan a trip

The app helps everyone agree on the trip and throw money into a shared pool every month until it’s time to go

Now go on TikTok and search “made it out the gc”

Filter by most viral videos, and from there I want you to religiously read every comment, every pain point, every complaint.

That’s your market. That’s literally it. You can do this in ANY niche. Doesn’t matter if you’re selling special toilet paper.

There’s an audience out there for you.

Once you’ve proven the demand, it’s time to go market the damn thing.

Some formats take longer than others. I’ve made hundreds of thousands of videos for companies, products, apps — everything you can think of.

What I will say is every single thing has its own angle and uniqueness.

Always test. There’s no secret theory on how to go viral.

It’s taking something that works in another industry and doubling down over and over until it sticks.

And let me say this loud and clear: do NOT be so damn attached to your views just because in the first week you’re not viral.

It does NOT always work like that.

I’ve grown pages from 0–50k in 3 days… but there have also been seasons where I couldn’t go viral for months. It happens. It’s the game.

My advice to anyone who wants to win is stay a student.

I’m ALWAYS in the trenches making content. It’s what I do. So I see both sides.

But yea… keep freaking going. There’s an audience out there who will buy from you. You just gotta find them.

Hopefully you made it this far lmao.

My hands hurt so I hope you enjoyed this as much as I did writing it.

cases.title: Personally, I don't feel like there is any need to warm up a channel. It feels like extra work when you just want to upload and start making money. But from experience, you stand a chance of getting a
cases.content:
Personally, I don't feel like there is any need to warm up a channel. It feels like extra work when you just want to upload and start making money. But from experience, you stand a chance of getting a low trust score if you refuse to do it, and that is not a risk I want to take anymore. Not after everything it takes to set up a faceless channel from scratch.

Repost this so someone will learn.

So let me talk to you like a friend for a minute, or like your father figure, whatever.

The moment you create a fresh YouTube account, YouTube is already studying you. It is asking questions you cannot hear. Who is this person? What do they like? Are they even real? And here is the painful truth. If you create an account today and upload a video ten minutes later, YouTube looks at that and thinks this smells like a bot. Your video gets flagged. Your channel gets low trust. And everything you worked hard to build starts on a broken foundation.

That is not what you want, right? You are going to walk in like we belong there. Calm. Steady. Real.

So here is what you do.

For the first three days, do not touch your channel settings. Do not upload anything. Just act like a normal person who loves watching YouTube. Watch about 30 minutes of content in your niche every single day. If your channel is about motivation, watch motivation videos. If it is about scary stories, watch scary story videos. YouTube is paying attention to every second of this. It is building a picture of who you are and what your account cares about.

While you are watching, like a few videos. Not all of them. Just a few that you actually enjoy. Random scattered likes look more human and that is exactly what you want YouTube to see.

Then leave 2 to 3 real comments. This is the part most people skip and it is one of the most powerful steps. Write something that actually makes sense. Something like, this breakdown really helped me understand this better or I never thought of it this way before. YouTube sees an account that is engaging with the community and it begins to trust you.

Also subscribe to about 3 to 5 channels in your niche. This completes the picture. You are not just a visitor anymore. You are a member of this community. YouTube's algorithm starts to understand what world your account belongs to.

Do this for three days straight. I know it feels slow. I know you are excited and you just want to start uploading. But think of it this way. You are planting a seed before you expect a harvest. Every big creator you admire started somewhere. You are just doing it smarter than most of them did.

On day 4, now you can start setting up your channel. Upload your banner and logo. It does not have to be perfect. It just has to exist. A channel with no banner screams new and abandoned. Give YouTube and future viewers something to look at. Change your channel name to something that matches your niche. Write a short channel description. Two to three sentences is enough. Just say what the channel is about and who it is for. You can always come back and improve it later.

By the end of day 4, your channel should look like it belongs to someone who is serious about this thing.

Then on day 5, you upload your first video. And here is the beautiful part about doing it this way. Your channel no longer looks like a spam account that was created an hour ago. It looks like a real account with history and engagement and interest. YouTube already has data on you. You have given it something to work with. Your first video is walking into a room where people already have a reason to take you seriously.

Most people create an account and upload ten minutes later because they are excited and in a rush. They want results fast. And YouTube punishes them for it because the algorithm cannot tell them apart from bots and spam accounts that do the exact same thing.

But you are not most people. You are reading this because you want to build something real. Something that lasts. And the truth is, five days of patience at the beginning can save you months of frustration later.

So, what do you think? Is warming up a channel a waste of time?

cases.title:
Brutally honest truths about YouTube Automation NOBODY talks about (save this)

> You'll probably fail your first 2-5 channels, that's just the game. And most people quit after the first failure, but
cases.content:
Brutally honest truths about YouTube Automation NOBODY talks about (save this)

> You'll probably fail your first 2-5 channels, that's just the game. And most people quit after the first failure, but the ones making $100K+/month... they failed multiple times before figuring it out. Since if you don't quit, you literally can't lose

> Political/news channels can make $50K+/month but if you get banned, YouTube doesn't pay out. Even if you 'earned' the money. And you might screw up your entire AdSense account. High risk, high reward risk

> RPM matters WAY more than views. A channel with $25 RPM at 500K views/month makes $12.5K, and channel with $2 RPM needs 6.25M views/month for the same income

> Most people fail because they quit, not because their content sucks. Consistency beats talent every. single. time. Just keep showing up AND improving

> Low barrier = high competition. Anyone with $100 and WiFi can start, so yeah, it's getting more crowded, especially lower-quality content. But 90% upload trash or quit after 3 weeks. Outlast them and you win

> Treat this like a real business, not a side hustle. If you want $30K-$100K/month, you need to work 8+ hours/day

> Bad output = bad systems, not bad people. If your editor keeps messing up, it's because your SOPs suck. Build proper systems with Loom videos, guides, and FAQs or your team will always fail

> SOPs make you scalable. Without them, you're stuck in chaos as soon as you try to hire or scale. And good SOPs also make your channels sellable if you ever want to exit

> Diversify across multiple channels and niches. Relying on one channel is not smart

> Longer videos = more money. 8+ minute videos unlock mid-roll ads. 60+ minute videos = even more mid-rolls = higher RPM

> If no one clicks your thumbnail, no one watches. Doesn't matter how good your script or editing is. Packaging is literally 90% of the game

> You're not too late, you're too slow. Hundreds of faceless channels are blowing up RIGHT NOW with simple content. They're not early, they just take action while you're overthinking

> Copy formats that work, but add your own twist. Replicating proven formats is how you win. Just don't blindly copy everything. Use the structure, then make it unique

> Dub your videos in other languages. Same video, but dubbed in languages like Spanish, French, Italian, Portuguese, etc.
